message: br-bd-qdjs4 add btrfs write-to-directory EISDIR coverage

files.patch:
@@ -1009,7 +1009,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-pzc6","title":"Implement i_version increment-on-write for NFSv4 and statx(2) compatibility","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 inodes have a 64-bit version counter split across two fields: i_version (l_i_version at offset 0x24, low 32 bits) and i_version_hi (offset 0x98, high 32 bits). This counter is used by NFSv4 for cache coherence (the NFS change attribute) and by statx(2) via STATX_CHANGE_COOKIE. The kernel increments it on every inode metadata or data change via ext4_inode_set_iversion_raw().

CURRENT STATE: Commit 4a1fb8a added the version and version_hi fields to Ext4Inode and wired them into parse/serialize, so the values are preserved across read-modify-write cycles. However, we never INCREMENT them — every write-back produces the same version counter the inode had when we read it.

IMPACT: NFS clients mounting a FrankenFS-served ext4 image via NFS will never see version changes, causing them to serve stale cached data indefinitely. The statx(2) STATX_CHANGE_COOKIE field will also be stale.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. Add a helper function bump_inode_version(inode: &mut Ext4Inode) that increments the 64-bit version counter (version + version_hi as a combined u64, then split back).
2. Call bump_inode_version in every write path that modifies the inode: ext4_write, ext4_create, ext4_mkdir, ext4_unlink, ext4_rmdir, ext4_rename, ext4_link, ext4_symlink, ext4_setattr (truncate, chmod, chown, utimes), ext4_setxattr, ext4_removexattr.
3. Only bump when metadata_csum or 64bit features are enabled (the field is only meaningful with extended inodes).
4. Add a test that performs a write, reads back the inode, and verifies version > original_version.
5. Add a test that performs multiple writes and verifies the version is monotonically increasing.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Every inode-modifying operation bumps i_version.
- Version is monotonically increasing within a mount session.
- Round-trip test: write, read-back, verify version increased.
- No version bump on read-only operations (getattr, read, readdir).
- The 64-bit counter wraps correctly at u64::MAX (wrapping_add).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:38.971610895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:23.987199529Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:23.986685286Z","close_reason":"Added bump_inode_version() called from touch_mtime_ctime() and touch_ctime(). All 3,591 tests pass. Commit f3c69f2.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","inode","nfs"]}
 {"id":"bd-q0g","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement permission/uid/gid + mode mapping details [phased]","description":"Future task: ensure getattr reports correct permission bits and ownership, and FUSE enforces them where appropriate.

Deliverables:
- Map inode mode bits to POSIX file types + permissions.
- Surface uid/gid correctly (including high bits).

Acceptance:
- getattr output matches kernel for a fixture inode set.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PinkCreek","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:47.212225418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:20.184893543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:20.184870791Z","close_reason":"Implemented device_number/major/minor on Ext4Inode, wired rdev in inode_to_attr. uid/gid/permissions were already correct. 8 tests added.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q0g","depends_on_id":"bd-10t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.896707087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-q0g","depends_on_id":"bd-3qq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.815846651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-q31x","title":"Chunk tree expansion should error on invalid chunk items","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:10:52.630646933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:17:01.697396011Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:17:01.697204700Z","close_reason":"Chunk tree expansion now errors on invalid chunk items; added regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-qdjs4","title":"add mounted-path btrfs write-to-directory EISDIR coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E covers regular-file write behavior, but the public refusal for writing directly to a directory is still only exercised in ffs-core unit tests. Scope: add a btrfs mounted-path probe that attempts a write on a directory path or fd, asserts exact EISDIR, and verifies directory contents remain unchanged. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the btrfs write-to-directory public contract.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:15:27.161060134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:15:27.161060134Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the btrfs write-to-directory public contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:15:27.161060134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:42:20.334467435Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:42:20.331941963Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qeon","title":"Add btrfs transaction adversarial mutation coverage","description":"Add deterministic adversarial coverage for BtrfsTransaction mutation boundaries: same-tree root replacement persists only the last root, commit-time delayed-ref flush failure leaves no visible metadata/payload writes, and tree-root block address overflow does not leak partially staged transaction metadata. Update conformance/COVERAGE.md with the new transaction mutation surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T10:48:11.538919650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T10:58:17.470397861Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T10:58:17.468161208Z","close_reason":"Completed btrfs transaction adversarial mutation coverage; focused tests, workspace check, and workspace clippy passed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mutation","testing"]}

 {"id":"bd-qerj","title":"Fix FUSE GETFLAGS/SETFLAGS fileattr ABI width mismatch","description":"ffs-fuse currently handles GETFLAGS/SETFLAGS as 8-byte long payloads, but Linux FUSE fileattr_get/fileattr_set invokes FS_IOC_GETFLAGS/SETFLAGS with sizeof(unsigned int) buffers. Patch ioctl handling/tests so 4-byte fileattr buffers work correctly without breaking the existing command matching.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:57:34.073491405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T19:03:33.939917684Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T19:03:33.939625621Z","close_reason":"FUSE GETFLAGS/SETFLAGS now uses 4-byte fileattr-compatible buffers with regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -98,6 +98,8 @@ for create and mkdir attempts beneath a regular-file parent, with no visible
 root-entry or parent-file drift.
 Mounted-path ext4 write-path coverage now also freezes exact `EISDIR` for
 opening a directory for write, with no directory-entry drift and no child-file drift.
+Mounted-path btrfs write-path coverage now also freezes exact `EISDIR` for
+opening a directory for write, with no directory-entry drift and no child-file drift.
 Mounted-path btrfs namespace-refusal coverage now also freezes exact `ENOTDIR`
 for create and mkdir attempts beneath a regular-file parent, with no visible
 workspace-entry or parent-file drift.
